title: Cacalia psilostachya
intro: Cacalia psilostachya, also known as Korean buttonbush or Cacalia, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Cacalia psilostachya has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia psilostachya include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cacalia psilostachya is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Korean herbal remedies and supplements.
taxonomy: Cacalia psilostachya is classified within the plant family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Cacalia, which is a group of flowering plants. This species is native to East Asia and is commonly found in mountainous regions. The classification of Cacalia psilostachya is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, and family Asteraceae. It is a perennial plant that grows up to a certain height, producing small white flowers.

The flavonoids present in Cacalia psilostachya have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health.
